series_metadata.series_description: State WIC and projects maintain WIC records so that auditors, state and federal evaluators, and participants can have access to them when necessary. Records include, but are not limited to, information pertaining to financial operations, food delivery systems, food instrument issuance and redemption, equipment purchases and inventory, certification, nutrition education, including breastfeeding promotion and support, civil rights and fair hearing procedures.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Paper: All physical documentation shall be kept three years and nine months past submission of final expenditure report which item pertains. If any litigation, claim, negotiation, audit or other action involving records has been started before end of three-year and nine month period, records shall be kept all issues are resolved, or end of regular three-year period, whichever is later. ; Electronic: All electronic documentation shall be kept three years and nine months past submission of final expenditure report which item pertains. If any litigation, claim, negotiation, audit or other action involving records has been started before end of three-year and nine month period, records shall be kept all issues are resolved, or end of regular three-year period, whichever is later
